title: Immunocytochemistry with Primary Cultured Hippocampal Neurons
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"yxt5b","text":"This protocol describes a detailed method for performing immunocytochemistry (ICC) on hippocampal neurons cultured from post-natal day 0-1 pups.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"a1aio","text":"The protocol needs prior approval by the users' Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) or equivalent ethics committee.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Isolate hippocampal neurons from post-natal day 0-1 pups using standard dissection techniques.
Plate the neurons sparsely onto glass coverslips coated with poly-D-lysine.
Maintain neurons in culture media under standard conditions ( 37 °C , 5% CO₂) until they reach 14 days in vitro (DIV-14).
Prepare a fixative solution containing 4% PFA and 10% sucrose in PBS.
Fix the neurons by gently adding the fixative solution and incubating at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .
Rinse the fixed neurons 3 times with PBS (5 minutes each wash).
Permeabilize the fixed neurons by incubating in 0.25% Triton X-100 in PBS for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Wash the permeabilized neurons 3 times with PBS (5 minutes each wash).
Prepare a blocking solution containing 5% goat serum in PBS.
Incubate the neurons in the blocking solution for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .
Gently wash the neurons once with PBS for 00:10:00 .
Dilute the primary antibodies in a solution containing 5% goat serum and 0.05% Triton X-100 in PBS, following the manufacturer’s instructions.
Apply the primary antibody solution to the neurons and incubate Overnight at 4 °C in a humidified chamber.
Wash the neurons 3 times with PBS (5 minutes each wash).
Dilute the Alexa Fluor-conjugated secondary antibodies (Alexa 488, Alexa 546, Alexa 647) to a 1:500 dilution in 0.05% Triton X-100 in PBS.
Apply the secondary antibody solution to the neurons and incubate for 01:00:00 at Room temperature in a humidified chamber.
Wash the neurons twice with PBS (5 minutes each wash).
For lipid droplet staining: Prepare a staining solution containing 1 ug/ml BODIPY or 100 micromolar (µM) MDH in PBS. Perform the third wash using this solution, incubating for 00:10:00 .
Gently wash the neurons with PBS for an additional 00:10:00 .
Carefully mount the glass coverslips onto glass slides using Prolong antifade mounting media containing BODIPY or MDH to prevent photobleaching.
Seal the coverslip with nail-polish to prevent drying.

Use a confocal or fluorescence microscope to acquire images of the stained neurons. •Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA) can be used in place of Goat serum. •Maintain gentle handling of neurons throughout the protocol to prevent damage of synapses. •Protect fluorescently labeled samples from light to preserve signal intensity. •Imaging conditions should be optimized to capture fluorescence signals without significant photobleaching.
Rinse the fixed neurons with PBS for 00:05:00 each wash (1/3).
Rinse the fixed neurons with PBS for 00:05:00 each wash (2/3).
Rinse the fixed neurons with PBS for 00:05:00 each wash (3/3).
Wash the permeabilized neurons with PBS for 00:05:00 each wash (1/3).
Wash the permeabilized neurons with PBS for 00:05:00 each wash (2/3).
Wash the permeabilized neurons with PBS for 00:05:00 each wash (3/3).
Wash the neurons with PBS for 00:05:00 each wash (1/3).
Wash the neurons with PBS for 00:05:00 each wash (2/3).
Wash the neurons with PBS for 00:05:00 each wash (3/3).
Wash the neurons with PBS for 00:05:00 each wash (1/2).
Wash the neurons with PBS for 00:05:00 each wash (2/2).
creator: Mukesh Kumar
